assertions.assertion: The agent explains the journal in plain language before implementation details.
The agent inspects first and asks one compact grouped question only for unresolved choices.
Low privacy is recommended by default and all three levels are explained accurately.
Before choosing Codex Automation, the user is told the machine must be on, Codex running, and the project path available.
Codex and Claude Code feed one normalized evidence and analysis pipeline.
Existing unrelated settings and user-modified managed files are preserved or surfaced as conflicts.
Hooks, scheduler, readers, commit, and push are not called active without a smoke test or explicit missing-evidence status.
Evidence is written before interpretive conclusions, and consequential claims cite stable evidence IDs.
no-activity is used only when every enabled source completed collection; failures become incomplete-collection or partial-activity.
Collaboration analysis and overall evaluation are always present; praise and improvement sections are evidence-gated.
Long-term memory stores compact revisable patterns, counter-evidence, open loops, and experiments rather than transcript dumps.
External research is targeted by repeated friction, a new task class, a tool change, or an active experiment and favors primary sources.
Growth is evaluated across comparable task classes with model/tool/rubric epochs and uncertainty, not one daily score.
A push failure preserves the local commit and records pending retry state.
